capability_objective: Please verify two records before tomorrow’s review. Search separately for “Chicago rail fare — outreach trip” in Chicago and “Boston team lunch — budget workshop” in Boston, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete expense records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Perform the read-only expense check in the supplied sandbox. Read README.md first, then use the genuine local `./expense-ledger` executable through Pi's Bash tool as the sole interface to expense records and notifications. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or generated runtime data, or its audit evidence. Do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under `.harness`; running the verification command named below is the only permitted access to that directory. Do not manufacture execution evidence or create a helper script, function, loop, wrapper, or scratch file.

Locate the exact expense description `Chicago rail fare — outreach trip` in `Chicago` and the exact expense description `Boston team lunch — budget workshop` in `Boston`. Every reported value must be grounded by first searching the ledger and then retrieving the complete current record by the unique stable ID returned by that expense's search. The searches are independent: start them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct ledger operation in each call. Do not combine them in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both results. Continue only if each search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either search is missing, ambiguous, or lacks an ID, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved lookup without guessing, and do not create the report.

Only after both searches have completed successfully, retrieve the two complete records using only their respective returned IDs. Start the retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that accesses expense data, again with one direct ledger operation in each call. Do not begin either retrieval until both searches have finished. If either retrieval fails, does not correspond to its search result, or lacks a status or date, stop and do not create the report. Base the comparison and every report value only on the two complete records returned by retrieval, never on search summaries, this request, or prior knowledge.

This check is strictly read-only. Invoke no ledger operation except the two required searches and the two eligible retrievals. Do not create, update, cancel, or notify; do not alter requested, distractor, archived, or related records, notifications, backing or runtime data, audit evidence, or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events. Apart from `expense-review.md` and runtime files created automatically by the required ledger reads, do not create, modify, or delete any file or directory.

When both complete records are retrieved successfully, create `expense-review.md` as the only file you write directly. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated, nonblank plain-text sentences with no heading, bullets, or extra prose. Put the Chicago expense first and the Boston expense second. Format the first two lines exactly as `<description> in <city> (<stable_id>) has status <status> and date <date>.`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by each complete record. Format the third line exactly as `Comparison: statuses <match-or-differ> (<Chicago-status> versus <Boston-status>), and dates <match-or-differ> (<Chicago-date> versus <Boston-date>).`, replacing each `match-or-differ` with `match` when the corresponding returned values are equal and `differ` otherwise. Include no other fields, inference, recommendation, or action. Run `python3 -B .harness/verify.py` after writing the report. Return exactly the report content as the final response.
